points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Minutes for the October 12, 2022 meeting were delivered to committee members and posted on the City Hall bulletin board; after offering public comment, the committee approved the minutes via roll call vote with unanimous yes responses.
points.source: Toni Hsu : Agenda item 3 minutes Approval minutes for the October 12th, 2022 meeting have been delivered to committee members and posted on the City Hall bulletin board.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Committee members present included Cliff Bryant, Susie Mehr, Regina Cycles, Tutu Thompson, Don Whitebread, Vice chair Hsu, with Chair Jesse Kopp arriving late.
points.source: John Lang : Committee member Cliff Bryant here. Susie Mehr. Thank you. Regina Cycles. Tutu Thompson. Thank you Don Whitebread. Here. Thank you and Vice chair Hsu. Here. And chair Jesse Kopp is currently not here.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: A public attendee reported difficulty connecting from agenda PDFs because Zoom codes were not embedded. Staff stated the issue has been raised with document processing and committed to work on resolving the inconsistency.
points.source: John Lang : I've raised it with document processing Bruce, and I'll work on getting it resolved, so thank you.

points.speaker: Naya
points.text: Local artist introduced herself as Mountain View High School 2019 graduate currently studying art and history in Rhode Island, interested in learning about public art processes.
points.source: Naya : I graduated from Mountain View High School in 2019, so 3-4 years ago or something like that at that point, this point. And I'm current students in Rhode Island studying art as well as history.

points.speaker: Naya
points.text: Proposed future project responding to El Camino bells that would create dialogue with existing monuments without toppling them, specifically near Castro Street bell.
points.source: Naya : So my idea kind of with this project is to make a piece which I have photos and things of which I could send to the committee later, that, yeah, it takes on like these kind of plays with the idea of a toppled monument, but without actually toppling the monument.

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: A committee member expressed support for VAC involvement in the pedestrian mall functional plan and emphasized interest in discussing the next public art strategy iteration, including initiatives, processes, and funding mechanisms.
points.source: Susi Merhar : …very happy to hear John that gonna work on a comprehensive approach towards the pedestrian mall… I hope we can talk about the public art strategy more, especially about the initiatives and how that all ties in and what processes and and funding will work.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Requested agenda item to re-examine process for selecting public art in parks and better integration with play equipment selection.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : So this Cliff, one topic I'd like to that I mentioned the last meeting was kind of re looking at our process for selecting public art that goes into a public park and how that ties in with the art.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Given Clerk direction, the committee opted not to vote on the AB 361 resolution. The December meeting will occur virtually under Council's extension, and meetings will transition to in-person beginning in February.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : It's just gonna like, take… time… and not accomplish anything. So we're going to 7.2.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Committee will likely meet in atrium room which allows hybrid meetings, while Rankstorff Community Center lacks hybrid capability.
points.source: John Lang : The atrium is actually a hybrid room would allow for hybrid meetings. Unfortunately, the Rank Store Center does not allow for hybrid right at the present time.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee member expressed concern about transition to in-person meetings affecting participation for members with families.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : Where it's been nice though is that it's been able to you know people with with families and whatnot to participate in the need these meetings and it's going to be more difficult for you know as a result.

topics.topic: Public art placement and process improvements

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff identified need for process improvements after Pyramid Park deployment, recognizing scale and proximity issues requiring new approach.
points.source: John Lang : So as committee member Bryant mentioned in upcoming Agenda topics, this was a topic I think that came up last committee meeting on really trying to understand after seeing Pyramid Park in the the deployment of the art, recognizing maybe there's some things need to change.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Playground equipment information will be shared with committee since it's typically already sourced before art selection, including color palette and design details.
points.source: John Lang : So making sure that that information is also conveyed from a colour palette from a look and feel. So as these projects go forward, typically playground structures are actually already either completely designed or already in the process of procurement.

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: Suggested pre-selecting larger pool of finalists to allow for detailed discussions about dimensions and placement before final selection.
points.source: Susi Merhar : In the sense of that we probably should pre select a larger pool and then narrow down just so that we can apply all those all that extra information and that we don't influence the artists beforehand

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Committee emphasized importance of providing stipends when requesting multi-step process with additional work from finalist artists.
points.source: Toni Hsu : But we want some room and some some opportunity to work with the individual artists. And then we're so not asking. We're not only asking for their time, right, and their commitment, but we're also asking for them to essentially create.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff suggested threshold-based approach where smaller projects use straightforward process while larger projects employ iterative stipend-based selection.
points.source: John Lang : So maybe there's a threshold requirement because I think for the park that is extremely small where you're talking, it just minimally triggers the CIP project where it might just be benches. This iterative process seems a little and I think committee member White Bread was kind of on to this.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee agreed threshold should apply per art piece rather than total CIP project amount to allow appropriate process selection.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : But I'm thinking if there's a threshold, the threshold should probably apply for piece and not for the total amount of the CIP project.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Current process attaches artist's original submission to contract without additional schematics, with no iterative changes after City Council approval.
points.source: John Lang : So what ends up happening once the VAC approves, approves an art piece, it goes to City Council. But basically the submittal that the artist provided gets to be an attachment to the artist contract.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee member noted discrepancies between approved submissions and final installed pieces, requesting checkpoint process during fabrication.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : What I've seen is that what's submitted to us and what we finally see installed may may differ and and and hence there's no and there's no checkpoint along the way.

topics.topic: Center for the Performing Arts call

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: 2019 call for artists generated 41 respondents, providing context for current 29 submissions with three days remaining.
points.source: John Lang : Relative to last year the the call. Was. The the 2019 call, which was the last call that was done, had 41 respondents.

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Committee member requested clarification on protocols for responding to direct public inquiries about Visual Arts Committee processes.
points.source: Toni Hsu : I have one question and I just I it would be helpful to know what the protocol is when general public reaches out to us directly with questions or comments or wanting to understand the inner workings on the visual arts Committee.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Jesse Cupp thanked John Lang for keeping committee moving in right direction despite staff transitions.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : I think we're in really, really good hands with John, even though Tiffany's not here anymore. I think we got a great staff member who we can trust to keep things going in the right direction. So thanks, John.

decisions.text: Decline to take action on the AB 361 resolution, relying on Council's extension for December and planning for in-person meetings from February 1, 2023.
decisions.source: Jesse Cupp : It's just gonna… not accomplish anything. So we're going to 7.2.

decisions.text: Work with document processing to resolve inconsistent Zoom links in agenda PDFs.
decisions.source: John Lang : I've raised it with document processing Bruce, and I'll work on getting it resolved…

decisions.text: Staff will explore how to engage residents who participated in park equipment selection into public art selection process.
decisions.source: John Lang : Happy to explore. I I need to kind of understand the the parks process a little bit better but how the VAC can engage with that already identified group.
